title: galium mollugo subsp. insubricum
intro: Galium mollugo subsp. insubricum is a subspecies of the sweet woodruff plant, a perennial herb belonging to the gentian family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits associated with Galium mollugo subsp. insubricum include relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and menstrual cramps, as well as its ability to soothe nervous system disorders. Herbal preparations of this plant may include teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal and health benefits.
